free_text: Nuclear data measurements for FR-wall and structural
materials
Only cr.sections for nuclear reactions induced by fast
neutrons produced in the break-up 30 MeV deuterons
on beryllium are presented here.
Final data for reactions at En=14.7 MeV  published
in NP/A,283,269,1977 (see REL-REF, Entry 20721).
The Karlsruhe Cyclotron was used for
the deuteron  breakup neutrons.
Institut fuer Chemie of the Kernforschungs-
Anlage,Juelich.
Fast neutrons were produced by bombarding a
1cm thick Be-target with 30MeV deuterons at 2 microA
current, measured by a Faraday cup.
No fast pneumatic system used (comment
from Prof. S.M.Qaim)
Gamma-and X-ray-spectroscopy were employed.
Gamma-gamma coincidence.
High purity enriched samples were used.
Co-axial 28 cm**3 GeLi detector in case of
very low energy gamma-rays, or Ge detector of active
area 2 cm**2 with thin beryllium window.
Deuteron break-up spectrum has an approximate
Maxwellian shape with a maximum intensity at 11.5 MeV.
The shape of spectrum calculated by the method of
Schmidt and Muenzel is in Fig.1 of 76GARMIS.
EN-MIN about 2.0 MeV.
EN-MAX about 28. MeV .
Neutron flux densities obtained via the two methods
(exp. and calc.) were found to be almost identical.
- calc. of neutr.flux;
Method of Schmidt and Muenzel was used for the shape
spectrum calculation.
References for neutron spectra exp.measurements for
various incident deuterons energies.
Final
data for reactions at 14.7 MeV
For neutron flux monitoring
Excitation function
has been evaluated ( in MONIT-REF ) over the major
portion of neutron spectrum encountered in this work.
Usual corrections for:
- Decay,
- Gamma-transition intensities,
- Internal conversion coefficients,
- Counting efficiency,
- Geometry,
- Absorption.
Between 6 and 15 % were the errors.
Table 2 of Conference Proceeding 76GARMIS.
